apparatus_calibration_status: effective pupil, analyser-port gains, background and registration remain to be measured
atomic_response_status: ideal fully spin-polarised axial 166Er response with kappa_F=-45/91

canonical_context.power_exposure_note: The plotted quantities are noiseless intensities normalised to I0 and the dual-port normalised difference S, so probe power and exposure do not set the displayed values.

caption: Representative noiseless finite-aperture readouts of the reference condensate at |Delta|/2pi=1.5 GHz, imaged along x through the common effective NA=0.130 pupil. Panels (a)-(c) show image-plane intensity normalised to I0; panels (b) and (c) use the same intensity scale. Panel (d) shows the signed dual-port signal S=(I_H-I_V)/(I_H+I_V), whose negative central value follows the stated H/V convention. Camera sampling, detector noise and condensate evolution are omitted.
figure_type: canonical noiseless finite-aperture single-frame readout comparison
pipeline: scalar phase and ideal 166Er Faraday rotation maps
PCI, DGI, dark-field Faraday, and dual-port Faraday readouts

plotted_quantities.DGI: noiseless image-plane intensity I/I0 with OD_s=4 residual carrier

port_mapping: I_H = historical notebook I_v; I_V = historical notebook I_u
